actualDailyBurn.note: From Anthropic CSV ground truth in cost-state.json
actualDailyBurn.taskContextClaimNote: Task brief stated ~$400/day average May 10-14. Actual Anthropic CSV data shows ~$185/day average. The $400 figure may reflect earlier peak period (Apr 25-29: $50-$339/day spikes) or a different measurement window.

discrepancyNote: Based on actual Anthropic CSV data, tracker is closer to accurate than the $400 claim suggested. However, on high-work days (May 12: $295), the tracker underestimates by ~2x due to subagent and isolated-session cost not being counted in real-time.
discrepancyRootCauses: Isolated cron sessions (Blog: 326s Sonnet, Standup: 332s Sonnet, AKB Holocron: 241s Sonnet) run as separate isolated sessions — not counted in real-time turn tracker
Subagent turns spawned from interactive sessions (TKT work, investigations like this one) counted separately from main session turn tracker
Cost tracker samples main session turn count and applies average cost/turn — misses isolated session token overhead
Dashboard sessions (standup origin, journal incremental) have their own context loading not reflected in main session cost
High-cost days (May 10: $266, May 12: $295) correlate with heavy interactive Ken sessions + complex subagent work — not cron layer

quickWinsApplied.name: Aria Daily Summary
quickWinsApplied.rationale: Medium-currency work (structured session history reading + markdown write). No creative judgment needed. Haiku handles perfectly.

quickWinsApplied.name: Daily Blog
quickWinsApplied.rationale: Creative + governance work justifies Sonnet. But missing lightContext was adding unnecessary bootstrap context (~40K tokens) to every isolated session.

quickWinsApplied.name: Drive Sync
quickWinsApplied.rationale: Simple script-run + conditional branch. Haiku correct. lightContext reduces context overhead.

note: Cron layer quick wins are modest (~$0.52/day). The dominant cost drivers are interactive Ken sessions and complex subagent work, not the cron layer.

realCostDrivers.finding1: Interactive sessions on peak work days are the PRIMARY cost driver. May 12 ($295) and May 10 ($266) correlated with heavy TKT work (MinIO, Sandbox, subagent spawning).
realCostDrivers.finding2: The cron layer is largely well-optimized already. 12 systemEvents (zero cost), 14 Haiku/lightContext crons, 7 Ollama-free crons. Only 4 Sonnet crons and 3 were justified.
realCostDrivers.finding3: The obs.db shows delegation source with 3761 entries ALL at ERROR level — this may indicate the delegation logger writes at ERROR by default, not that 3761 events failed. Needs verification.
realCostDrivers.finding4: Warden (857 entries, all ERROR-level in obs.db) — same pattern. Likely logging convention, not real errors.
realCostDrivers.finding5: Daily Burn Alert (ca5d5e50) had consecutiveErrors: 1 — agent couldn't generate response. Likely a state file shape mismatch. Monitor.

high.description: Creative synthesis, governance judgment, multi-source integration

medium.description: Structured output, conditional routing, read+summarize patterns

low.description: Script run + report, simple conditional, structured data fetch

topLLMCrudOffenders.issue: Was running Sonnet for a CRUD-like read-session-history → write-markdown task

topLLMCrudOffenders.name: Daily Blog (missing lightContext)
topLLMCrudOffenders.issue: High-complexity creative work but was loading full 40K+ token bootstrap context unnecessarily

topLLMCrudOffenders.name: Aria Weekly ROI Summary
topLLMCrudOffenders.issue: Sonnet for read-files → compose-summary task. Medium currency work misclassified as High

workCurrencyRouting.trigger: Output requires synthesis, judgment, or multi-source reasoning

workCurrencyRouting.workType: Medium (structured output, conditional routing, compliance checks)
workCurrencyRouting.trigger: Output is structured (markdown/JSON), single-source, conditional logic
Run script → capture exit code → conditional message

recommendedImplementation: Gate every new cron creation with Work Currency classification (High/Medium/Low/None) as mandatory field
Default new agentTurn crons to Haiku + lightContext unless explicitly classified High
Monthly review: any Sonnet cron not marked High must have Ken sign-off
Convert Aria Weekly ROI to Haiku (pending Ken approval)
Add lightContext to Monthly Model Strategy Review cron (low urgency, monthly)

recommendationsForKen.title: Aria Weekly ROI Summary: Sonnet → Haiku
recommendationsForKen.detail: Weekly ROI summary is medium-currency work (read files → compose plain-language summary). Haiku handles this. Estimated saving: ~$1.60/month. Needs your approval before applying.

recommendationsForKen.title: Real cost driver is interactive sessions, not cron layer
recommendationsForKen.detail: Peak days (May 10: $266, May 12: $295) are driven by heavy interactive Ken+Yoda sessions with subagent spawning. Cron layer is already well-optimized. To meaningfully cut costs, need to implement Work Currency routing at the INTERACTIVE level — routing medium-complexity Ken tasks to Haiku first.
recommendationsForKen.action: TKT-0165 implementation of interactive routing model

recommendationsForKen.title: Daily Burn Alert cron errored — monitor
recommendationsForKen.detail: ca5d5e50 (Daily Burn Alert at 20:00) had consecutiveErrors: 1 — 'agent couldn't generate a response'. Likely a cost-state.json shape issue. Auto-heals on next run but worth watching.

recommendationsForKen.title: Weekly Asset Review uses ~ path in prompt
recommendationsForKen.detail: The asset review cron (e8b17c79) calls bash ~/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/asset-review.sh — this tilde will fail in isolated sessions per RULES.md. Not a cost issue but a reliability issue.
recommendationsForKen.action: Raise separate TKT or fold into next sprint infra cleanup
